proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with stage_iv == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with stage_iv == 0.
Patients with has_brain_mets == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with has_brain_mets == 0.
Patients with liver_mets == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with liver_mets == 0.
Patients with bone_mets == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than patients with bone_mets == 0.

analyses.result_summary: Stage IV: mean PFS 3.617 mo (n=14969) vs non-stage-IV 5.153 (n=35031); difference -1.536 mo, Welch t-test p=0.
Brain mets: mean PFS 3.750 (n=5090) vs no brain mets 4.800 (n=44910); difference -1.050 mo, p=1.45e-178.
Liver mets: mean PFS 4.708 vs 4.690; difference +0.018 mo, p=0.555. No detectable effect.
Bone mets: mean PFS 4.723 vs 4.685; difference +0.038 mo, p=0.17. No detectable effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps (1, 2 vs 0) is associated with shorter pfs_months in a monotonic dose-response manner.
Patients with node_positive == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than node-negative patients.

analyses.result_summary: ECOG=1 vs 0: -1.196 mo (p=0); ECOG=2 vs 0: -2.351 mo (p=0). Strong monotonic worsening with worse PS.
Node positive: mean PFS 4.681 vs 4.705; difference -0.024 mo, p=0.292. No detectable effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with er_positive == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than ER-negative patients.
Patients with pr_positive == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than PR-negative patients.
Patients with her2_positive == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than HER2-negative patients (more aggressive disease unless effectively targeted).
Patients with her2_low == 1 have different mean pfs_months than HER2-zero patients.

analyses.code: ttest ER+ vs ER- on pfs

analyses.result_summary: HER2+: 4.317 vs HER2-: 4.776; diff -0.459 mo, p=6.1e-70 — HER2+ patients have shorter PFS.
HER2-low: 4.765 vs HER2-0: 4.650; diff +0.114 mo, p=1.04e-06 — small but significant longer PFS for HER2-low.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_tamoxifen == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than untreated patients overall.
The PFS benefit of treatment_tamoxifen is greater in er_positive == 1 patients than in er_positive == 0 patients (positive treatment×ER interaction on pfs_months).

analyses.result_summary: Tamoxifen: 4.723 vs 4.680; diff +0.043 mo, p=0.0769. No clear main effect.

Interaction coefficient treatment_tamoxifen:er_positive = -0.058 mo (p=0.27). Subgroup tests: ER+ on/off tam diff=+0.023 (p=0.45); ER- on/off tam diff=+0.081 (p=0.03). No significant tamoxifen×ER interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_palbociclib == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than untreated patients overall.
The PFS benefit of treatment_palbociclib is restricted to er_positive == 1 patients (positive treatment×ER interaction; no benefit in er_positive == 0).
The PFS benefit of treatment_palbociclib is greater in postmenopausal == 1 than postmenopausal == 0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Palbociclib: 5.408 vs 4.310; diff +1.098 mo, p=0. Large positive main effect.
Palbociclib×ER+ interaction = +1.594 mo (p=9.31e-229). ER+ on/off palbo diff=+1.577 (p=0); ER- on/off palbo diff=-0.017 (p=0.627). Benefit confined to ER+.
Palbociclib×postmenopausal interaction = -0.134 mo (p=0.00429). Slightly smaller benefit in postmenopausal — opposite of the prespecified hypothesis direction (effect direction negative).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_trastuzumab == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than untreated patients overall.
The PFS benefit of treatment_trastuzumab is greater in her2_positive == 1 patients than her2_positive == 0 patients (positive treatment×HER2 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Trastuzumab: 4.645 vs 4.705; diff -0.060 mo, p=0.0292. Small negative main effect (likely confounded by HER2+ being a poorer-prognosis subset).

Trastuzumab×HER2+ interaction = -0.028 mo (p=0.695). HER2+ on/off trast diff=-0.079 (p=0.153); HER2- on/off diff=-0.051 (p=0.104). NO significant HER2-specific benefit detected — refutes the directional hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_olaparib == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than untreated patients overall.
The PFS benefit of treatment_olaparib is greater in BRCA-mutated patients (brca1_mutation==1 OR brca2_mutation==1) than in BRCA wild-type patients (positive olaparib×BRCA interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Olaparib main effect: 4.660 vs 4.696; diff -0.036 mo, p=0.33. No main effect.

Olaparib×BRCA-any interaction = +0.402 mo (p=0.0208). BRCA+ on/off olap diff=+0.346 mo (p=0.0414); BRCA- on/off diff=-0.056 (p=0.143). Significant positive interaction — olaparib benefit confined to BRCA carriers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than untreated patients overall.
The PFS benefit of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan is greater in her2_low == 1 patients (positive treatment×HER2-low interaction).
The PFS benefit of treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan is greater in TNBC patients (er_positive==0 & pr_positive==0 & her2_positive==0) than non-TNBC.

analyses.result_summary: SG main effect: 4.686 vs 4.694; diff -0.007 mo, p=0.846. No effect.
SG×HER2-low interaction = -0.038 mo (p=0.626). HER2-low subgroup on/off SG diff=-0.031 (p=0.639). NO HER2-low-specific benefit.
SG×TNBC interaction = -0.105 mo (p=0.246). TNBC on/off SG diff=-0.085 (p=0.217). NO TNBC-specific benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than untreated patients overall.
The PFS benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab is greater in TNBC patients than in non-TNBC patients (positive treatment×TNBC interaction).
The PFS benefit of treatment_pembrolizumab is greater in msi_high == 1 patients than in msi_high == 0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab main effect: 4.630 vs 4.704; diff -0.074 mo, p=0.0175. Small negative main effect (likely confounded by harder-to-treat populations).
Pembrolizumab×TNBC interaction = +0.185 mo (p=0.0152). Significant positive interaction supporting greater benefit in TNBC.
Pembrolizumab×MSI-high interaction = +0.217 mo (p=0.466). MSI-high pembro vs no diff=+0.141 (p=0.637). MSI-high subgroup very small (n=498); not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with shorter pfs_months across the cohort (Pearson correlation differs from 0).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months across the cohort (Pearson correlation differs from 0).
Higher nlr is associated with shorter pfs_months across the cohort (Pearson correlation differs from 0).
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with shorter pfs_months across the cohort (Pearson correlation differs from 0).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months across the cohort (Pearson correlation differs from 0).
Higher tumor_size_cm is associated with shorter pfs_months across the cohort (Pearson correlation differs from 0).
Higher ki67_pct is associated with shorter pfs_months across the cohort (Pearson correlation differs from 0).

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r(albumin_g_dl, pfs_months) = +0.1002, p=9.39e-112.
Pearson r(ldh_u_l, pfs_months) = -0.0164, p=0.000245.
Pearson r(nlr, pfs_months) = +0.0028, p=0.537.
Pearson r(crp_mg_l, pfs_months) = -0.0033, p=0.461.
Pearson r(weight_loss_pct_6mo, pfs_months) = -0.1155, p=4.07e-148.
Pearson r(tumor_size_cm, pfs_months) = +0.0004, p=0.931.
Pearson r(ki67_pct, pfs_months) = -0.0914, p=2.98e-93.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for stage_iv and ecog_ps, higher albumin_g_dl is independently associated with longer pfs_months.
After adjusting for stage_iv and ecog_ps, higher ldh_u_l is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.
After adjusting for stage_iv and ecog_ps, higher nlr is independently associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted albumin_g_dl coefficient = +0.493 mo per g/dL, p=3.6e-133. Independent positive effect.
Adjusted ldh_u_l coefficient = -0.00045 mo per U/L, p=0.000309. Small but independent negative effect.
Adjusted nlr coefficient = -0.0002 mo per unit, p=0.966. NLR has no independent association after adjustment — refutes hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months across the cohort.
Mean pfs_months differs between sex_female == 1 and sex_female == 0 patients in this cohort.
Mean pfs_months differs across race_ethnicity categories (disparities exist).
Mean pfs_months differs across insurance_type categories.
Patients with rural_residence == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than urban patients.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r(age_years, pfs_months) = +0.7015, p=0. STRONG POSITIVE correlation — older age is associated with LONGER PFS, the opposite of the prespecified direction.
Female: 4.686 (n=22371) vs male: 4.699 (n=27629); diff -0.013, p=0.562. No effect — note the breast-cancer cohort contains 55% males which is itself unusual.
All race_ethnicity coefficients have p>0.2 (vs asian reference). No detectable disparities by race.
All insurance_type coefficients have p>0.5 (vs medicaid reference). No disparities detected.
Rural: 4.714 vs urban: 4.688; diff +0.027, p=0.343. No effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with pik3ca_mutation == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than wild-type patients.
Patients with tp53_mutation == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than wild-type patients.
Patients with pten_loss == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than wild-type patients.
Patients with cdkn2a_loss == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than wild-type patients.
Patients with her2_amplification == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than wild-type patients.

analyses.result_summary: pik3ca_mutation: mean PFS 4.328 vs 4.887; diff -0.559 mo, p=6.07e-144.
tp53_mutation: mean PFS 4.697 vs 4.690; diff +0.007 mo, p=0.762.
pten_loss: mean PFS 4.656 vs 4.695; diff -0.039 mo, p=0.439.
cdkn2a_loss: mean PFS 4.695 vs 4.692; diff +0.003 mo, p=0.935.
her2_amplification: mean PFS 4.751 vs 4.691; diff +0.060 mo, p=0.382.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher fatigue_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher pain_nrs is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher dyspnea_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher cough_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher appetite_loss_grade is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r(fatigue_grade, pfs_months) = +0.0002, p=0.963. No detectable association.
Pearson r(pain_nrs, pfs_months) = -0.0016, p=0.72. No detectable association.
Pearson r(dyspnea_grade, pfs_months) = +0.0046, p=0.299. No detectable association.
Pearson r(cough_grade, pfs_months) = -0.0039, p=0.384. No detectable association.
Pearson r(appetite_loss_grade, pfs_months) = -0.0008, p=0.861. No detectable association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with diabetes_mellitus == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without.
Patients with hypertension == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without.
Patients with copd == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without.
Patients with chronic_kidney_disease == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without.
Patients with heart_failure == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without.
Patients with coronary_artery_disease == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without.
Patients with atrial_fibrillation == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without.
Patients with venous_thromboembolism_history == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without.
Patients with autoimmune_disease == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without.
Patients with depression_anxiety_diagnosis == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without.
Patients with prior_malignancy == 1 have shorter mean pfs_months than those without.

analyses.result_summary: diabetes_mellitus: 4.710 vs 4.688; diff +0.022 mo, p=0.413.
hypertension: 4.692 vs 4.694; diff -0.002 mo, p=0.913.
chronic_kidney_disease: 4.707 vs 4.691; diff +0.016 mo, p=0.642.
heart_failure: 4.707 vs 4.691; diff +0.016 mo, p=0.671.
coronary_artery_disease: 4.721 vs 4.687; diff +0.035 mo, p=0.231.
atrial_fibrillation: 4.683 vs 4.694; diff -0.011 mo, p=0.781.
venous_thromboembolism_history: 4.732 vs 4.690; diff +0.042 mo, p=0.377.
autoimmune_disease: 4.650 vs 4.695; diff -0.045 mo, p=0.377.
depression_anxiety_diagnosis: 4.669 vs 4.698; diff -0.030 mo, p=0.302.
prior_malignancy: 4.651 vs 4.699; diff -0.047 mo, p=0.166.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with prior_chemotherapy == 1 have different mean pfs_months than those without (more prior treatment may indicate either heavier disease or better selection).
Patients with prior_radiation == 1 have different mean pfs_months than those without (more prior treatment may indicate either heavier disease or better selection).
Patients with prior_surgery == 1 have different mean pfs_months than those without (more prior treatment may indicate either heavier disease or better selection).
Patients with prior_immunotherapy == 1 have different mean pfs_months than those without (more prior treatment may indicate either heavier disease or better selection).
Patients with prior_targeted_therapy == 1 have different mean pfs_months than those without (more prior treatment may indicate either heavier disease or better selection).
Higher prior_lines_of_therapy is associated with shorter pfs_months (heavily pretreated patients do worse).
Longer years_since_diagnosis is associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: prior_chemotherapy: 4.694 vs 4.692; diff +0.002 mo, p=0.936.
prior_radiation: 4.725 vs 4.679; diff +0.045 mo, p=0.0637.
prior_surgery: 4.736 vs 4.670; diff +0.066 mo, p=0.00487.
prior_immunotherapy: 4.692 vs 4.693; diff -0.001 mo, p=0.98.
prior_targeted_therapy: 4.698 vs 4.692; diff +0.006 mo, p=0.866.
r = +0.0042, p=0.343. No detectable effect.
r = -0.0022, p=0.63. No detectable effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: TNBC patients receiving BOTH treatment_pembrolizumab == 1 AND treatment_sacituzumab_govitecan == 1 have longer mean pfs_months than other TNBC patients (combined immune + ADC benefit).

analyses.code: ttest TNBC pembro+SG vs TNBC neither/either alone
analyses.result_summary: In TNBC, dual pembro+SG (n=158): 4.298 vs others (n=10338): 4.305; diff -0.007 mo, p=0.969. No additive benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of treatment_palbociclib differs by ecog_ps (the magnitude is smaller in worse-PS patients).
The PFS effect of treatment_trastuzumab differs by ecog_ps category.

analyses.result_summary: palbo×ECOG=1 interaction = -0.021 (p=0.657); palbo×ECOG=2 = +0.035 (p=0.604). No interaction with ECOG — palbo benefit is uniform across PS.
trast×ECOG=1 interaction = +0.045 (p=0.435); trast×ECOG=2 = +0.039 (p=0.633). No interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In has_brain_mets == 1 patients, treatment_trastuzumab is associated with longer pfs_months (trast×brain_mets interaction is positive).

analyses.result_summary: trast×brain_mets interaction = +0.079 mo (p=0.384). Subgroup: brain-mets pts on/off trast diff=+0.014 (p=0.87). No CNS-specific benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable model adjusting for stage_iv, has_brain_mets, ECOG, albumin, LDH, NLR, biomarkers, and treatments, treatment_palbociclib remains independently associated with longer pfs_months.
In the same multivariable model, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, and ECOG remain the strongest negative predictors of pfs_months.
In the same model, tnbc independently predicts shorter pfs_months and er_positive predicts longer pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment_palbociclib coefficient = +1.104 mo (p=0). Confirms strong independent benefit.
Stage IV -1.530 (p=0); has_brain_mets -1.032 (p=4.05e-232); ECOG=2 -2.350 (p=0). Confirms.
tnbc -0.296 mo (p=5.55e-13); er_positive +0.345 mo (p=1.26e-22). Confirms both.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a multivariable model with prespecified treatment×biomarker interactions, treatment_palbociclib×er_positive remains the strongest positive interaction on pfs_months.
In the same model, treatment_olaparib×brca_any has a positive interaction on pfs_months after adjustment for clinical covariates.
In the same model, treatment_trastuzumab×her2_positive has a positive interaction on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: palbo×er_positive interaction = +1.596 mo, p=4.17e-296. Strongly supported.
olap×brca_any interaction = +0.179 mo, p=0.223. Direction positive but the adjusted interaction term loses significance after adding the full covariate adjustment.
trast×her2_positive interaction = -0.004 mo, p=0.953. Effectively zero — refutes hypothesis.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across the panel of pharmacogenomic SNPs (snp_*), at least one variant is associated with mean pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Top 5 SNPs: snp_rs1801131 diff=-0.054 (p=0.0255); snp_rs2298771 diff=+0.048 (p=0.0804); snp_rs1799983 diff=-0.037 (p=0.118); snp_rs662 diff=-0.033 (p=0.174); snp_rs1045642 diff=-0.029 (p=0.198). 1 of 24 SNPs reached p<0.05 — consistent with chance after multiple testing. No robust SNP–PFS signal.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_tamoxifen has a different PFS effect by menopausal status; specifically the benefit is greater in premenopausal (postmenopausal == 0) patients (negative tamoxifen×postmenopausal interaction).

analyses.result_summary: tam×postmen interaction = -0.043 mo (p=0.392). Subgroups: pre-meno on/off tam diff=+0.069 (p=0.0782); post-meno diff=+0.026 (p=0.399). Direction matches prior but interaction p>0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The PFS benefit of treatment_palbociclib is smaller in heavily pretreated patients (negative prior_lines_of_therapy × treatment_palbociclib interaction).

analyses.result_summary: prior_lines_of_therapy×treatment_palbociclib interaction = -0.024 mo per line (p=0.242). Direction negative but not significant — palbo benefit is essentially line-agnostic.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a parsimonious model with stage_iv, ECOG, albumin, LDH, NLR, weight loss, and prespecified treatment×biomarker interactions, the palbociclib×er_positive, trastuzumab×her2_positive, olaparib×brca_any, sacituzumab_govitecan×her2_low, and pembrolizumab×msi_high product terms are each positive and statistically significant.

analyses.result_summary: Significant POSITIVE interactions: palbociclib:er_positive +1.588 (p=0). trastuzumab:her2_positive -0.458 (p=2.95e-20) — NEGATIVE not positive (i.e. trastuzumab does NOT rescue HER2+ disease in this dataset). olaparib:brca_any +0.124 (p=0.368); sacituzumab_govitecan:her2_low +0.064 (p=0.203); pembrolizumab:msi_high +0.211 (p=0.355). Only palbociclib×ER+ is robustly supported; the other prespecified treatment×biomarker product terms in this compact model fail to reach significance, with trastuzumab×HER2+ frankly negative.
